main.270: GYUTRON combines smart cameras, white-light vision lighting, industrial sensors, barcode verification, edge control, and quality data workflows into inspection systems that help factories find defects earlier and protect downstream processes.
main.272: Built for inline inspection, assembly verification, traceability, and repeatable quality evidence across production lines.
main.278: From visual checks to traceable quality decisions.
main.279: Inspired by advanced industrial inspection ecosystems, this solution positions GYUTRON as more than a hardware supplier: cameras, sensing, code reading, lighting, controllers, and quality records are planned as one operational system.
main.280: Detect missing parts, wrong orientation, assembly errors, surface issues, height variance, and visible defects at production speed.
main.281: Read barcodes and DPM codes, validate labels, connect scan records to lots, and keep inspection evidence tied to each unit.
main.282: Use calibrated imaging, laser measurement, and inspection instruments for repeatable tolerance checks and appearance review.
main.283: Combine robot movement, fixed vision, and guided inspection points for final assembly, packaging, and multi-side product review.
main.285: Inspection hardware connected to factory decisions.
main.286: The architecture links image acquisition, sensor triggers, lighting control, edge analysis, PLC handshakes, and quality data capture so inspection results can stop defects before they travel downstream.
main.287: GYUTRON smart camera and inspection cell visual
main.289: Smart cameras, code readers, sensors, and stable white-light illumination capture reliable data at the inspection point.
main.291: Edge controllers and vision tools evaluate presence, position, dimensions, code quality, and surface conditions.
main.293: PLC signals, I/O modules, and robot or conveyor logic can reject, stop, route, or release units based on inspection outcomes.
main.295: Images, results, device events, and scan IDs can be structured for MES, WMS, dashboards, or customer quality reports.
main.297: A practical path from pilot station to repeatable line deployment.
main.298: GYUTRON solution work should start from the production problem, then map device choice, inspection logic, data interfaces, and operational handoff in a controlled sequence.
main.300: Clarify defect types, line speed, sample parts, reject logic, traceability records, and pass/fail ownership.
main.301: Select cameras, lighting, sensors, scanners, edge controllers, mounting, and interfaces around the physical station.
main.303: Run pilot checks, tune thresholds, document repeatability, and prepare the same architecture for adjacent stations.
main.304: Plan an inspection station with GYUTRON.
main.305: Share your part, defect examples, required throughput, lighting constraints, PLC/MES needs, and traceability goals. The GYUTRON team can recommend the right inspection stack and pilot route.

sol.measurement-3d-profiling.name: Measurement & 3D Profiling
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.seoTitle: Measurement & 3D Profiling Solutions | Inline Dimensional Gauging & Metrology | GYUTRON
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.seoDesc: Inline dimensional gauging, laser 3D profiling, and traceable metrology from GYUTRON. Sub-micron repeatability, 100% gauging at line speed, gauge-block-traceable results with full SPC logging.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.hero.title: Stop shipping out-of-tolerance parts
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.hero.desc: Hand calipers and sampling miss drift, deform soft parts, and leave no audit trail. GYUTRON gauges every part inline with telecentric optics and laser 3D profiling — height, gap, flatness, and form measured to sub-micron repeatability, traceable to gauge blocks, logged for SPC.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.hero.panelD: 2σ on telecentric optical gauges; laser profile Z-repeatability to 0.5 µm. Traceable to certified gauge blocks; results time-stamped per part for SPC and audit.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.gallery.int: Real captured profiles and gauging overlays from GYUTRON optical and laser measurement systems.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.shot.2.cap: 3D height map of connector lead row
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.shot.3.cap: Sealing-face deviation color map vs datum
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.shot.4.cap: Gap and step measured on stamped panel
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.shot.5.cap: Weld bead volume from snapshot profiler
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.shot.6.cap: Diameter run chart drifting toward limit
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.title: What the measurement layer does
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.intro: Non-contact gauging that replaces manual sampling — dimensional, height, and form data captured on every part, referenced to traceable standards.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.1.title: Telecentric Dimensional Gauging
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.1.desc: Edge, diameter, gap, and concentricity to ±0.8 µm 2σ. Distortion-free telecentric optics; no contact, no part deformation.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.2.title: Laser 3D Profiling
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.2.desc: Single-line and snapshot profilers reconstruct height, step, flatness, and warpage. Z-repeatability to 0.5 µm at full scan speed.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.3.title: Surface Form & Height
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.3.desc: Coplanarity, ball/lead height, weld bead, glue-line volume. Dense point clouds resolve features 2D imaging cannot reach.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.4.title: Traceable & SPC-Ready
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.4.desc: Gauge-block-referenced calibration with R&R support. Every result time-stamped to the part ID and streamed to SPC.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.arch.title: Sense → Profile → Gauge → Record
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.arch.intro: A measurement cell built from GYUTRON optics, lasers, and edge compute — calibrated to traceable standards and wired into your quality system end to end.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.arch.1.desc: Encoder or sensor triggers capture as the part enters frame. Telecentric optics and white-light or laser illumination freeze the feature without motion blur.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.arch.2.desc: Laser profiler sweeps the surface; edge controller fuses scans into a calibrated 3D point cloud and aligns it to the part datum.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.arch.3.desc: Dimensions extracted against the CAD-derived tolerance set. Pass / fail / rework decided in under 60 ms and signaled to the line PLC.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.arch.4.desc: Each measurement, overlay image, and verdict is logged to the part ID and pushed to SPC / MES for trend control and audit recall.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.title: From sample part to qualified gauge
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.intro: GYUTRON application engineers prove capability on your parts before rollout — gauge R&R signed off, not assumed.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.1.title: Feature & tolerance review
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.1.desc: We map critical dimensions, datums, and tolerance bands from the drawing, then select optics, laser, and field of view to suit.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.2.title: Capability study
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.2.desc: Run gauge R&R on your sample parts against certified standards. Confirm repeatability, bias, and cycle time before any line work.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.3.title: Integrate & qualify
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.wf.3.desc: Mount, calibrate, wire to PLC and SPC. Sign off measurement system analysis, then hand over with operator and recalibration procedures.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cases.title: Where inline measurement pays off
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cases.intro: Two representative deployments where sampling left defects in the field and 100% inline gauging closed the gap.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.case1.title: CNC turned parts gauged 100% inline
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.case1.metri: Diameter, length, and groove width measured to ±0.8 µm on every shaft; out-of-tolerance parts diverted before downstream assembly, replacing 1-in-50 hand-caliper sampling.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.case2.title: Connector coplanarity by laser profiling
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.case2.metri: Lead and ball height mapped across the full footprint; coplanarity over spec flagged at ~30 ms/part, catching bent leads that 2D vision missed.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.title: Sealing-face flatness gauged inline on a machined casting line
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.challeng: A powertrain machining line released castings on a 1-in-30 sample using hand depth gauges. Sealing-face flatness drift between samples reached the customer as leak failures, and contact gauging marked soft aluminum faces.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.approach: A GYUTRON laser profiler scanned each sealing face inline, reconstructing a 3D surface and computing flatness against the datum plane in under 50 ms. Telecentric gauges added bore diameter and bolt-hole spacing in the same cell. All results were referenced to certified gauge blocks and streamed to SPC keyed to the casting serial.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.result: 100% of faces gauged with 0.6 µm flatness repeatability. Flatness drift now triggers a tool-wear alarm before parts go out of tolerance, leak escapes dropped to near zero, and every casting carries a recallable measurement record.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cta.title: Send a sample part — we will prove the gauge
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cta.desc: Share the drawing, critical dimensions, tolerances, and cycle time. GYUTRON will recommend an optics-and-laser stack and run a capability study before you commit.
sol.identification-traceability.name: Identification & Traceability
sol.identification-traceability.seoTitle: Identification & Traceability | 1D/2D/DPM, OCR, RFID Track-and-Trace | GYUTRON
sol.identification-traceability.seoDesc: Read 1D/2D/DPM codes at 99.9%+ rates, decode OCR marks, link RFID tags, and build full part genealogy. GYUTRON code-reading cameras, scanners, RFID handhelds, and PDAs deliver end-to-end track-and-trace.
sol.identification-traceability.hero.tit: Every part read, linked, and recallable
sol.identification-traceability.hero.des: Low-contrast DPM, damaged labels, and high-speed lines push hand scanners and fixed readers past their limits — and a single no-read breaks the genealogy chain. GYUTRON reads 1D/2D/DPM codes, OCR text, and RFID tags, then links each ID into an end-to-end track-and-trace record from raw material to shipped unit.
sol.identification-traceability.hero.pan: Direct part marks on metal, PCB, glass, and curved surfaces — decoded under varying contrast, glare, and tilt at full line speed.
Representative captured frames across symbologies, surfaces, and capture modes.
sol.identification-traceability.shot.1.c: Dot-peen Data Matrix decoded on machined steel
sol.identification-traceability.shot.2.c: Lot and date string read off molded plastic
sol.identification-traceability.shot.3.c: Bulk UHF tag read on a loaded pallet
sol.identification-traceability.shot.4.c: Linked unit-to-assembly trace tree on PDA
sol.identification-traceability.cap.intr: One platform reads codes, OCR, and RFID, then writes a linked genealogy record — no-reads flagged, never lost.
sol.identification-traceability.cap.1.de: Data Matrix, QR, PDF417, and all common 1D codes. Dot-peen and laser DPM on metal, PCB, and curved parts decoded under glare and tilt.
sol.identification-traceability.cap.2.de: Reads printed and engraved lot, date, and serial strings. Verifies characters against expected format; rejects malformed or missing marks.
sol.identification-traceability.cap.3.de: UHF/HF RFID handhelds and rugged scanners for cartons, totes, and pallets. Bulk-read tagged loads where line-of-sight to a label fails.
sol.identification-traceability.cap.4.de: Each read links part, process, operator, and parent assembly. Full forward/backward trace; scoped recall by lot, shift, or component batch.
sol.identification-traceability.arch.int: A five-stage genealogy pipeline turns every captured ID into a permanent, queryable trace record — the core strength of an integrated identification system.
sol.identification-traceability.arch.1.d: Fixed code readers, handheld scanners, and RFID capture 1D/2D/DPM codes, OCR marks, and tags at every station — line speed, any surface.
sol.identification-traceability.arch.2.d: Decoded data is grade-checked and format-validated. Print quality (ISO/IEC 15415/15416), OCV match, and duplicate-ID checks gate the read.
sol.identification-traceability.arch.3.d: Each verified ID is bound to its part, process parameters, operator, station, and parent assembly — building the unit-to-product relationship.
sol.identification-traceability.arch.4.d: Linked events stream to MES/ERP and the trace database. Time-stamped, immutable, and indexed for audit and compliance retention.
sol.identification-traceability.arch.5.d: Query any unit's full history forward and backward. Scope containment to exact lots, shifts, or component batches — recall only what is affected.
sol.identification-traceability.cases.in: Representative deployments across industries that demand unit-level identification and full traceability.
sol.identification-traceability.case1.me: Dot-peen Data Matrix on cast and machined components read at each station to enforce error-proof routing and full genealogy.
sol.identification-traceability.case2.me: 2D codes on PCBs read pre- and post-reflow, linking each board to test, AOI, and panel-of-origin records.
sol.identification-traceability.case3.me: Unit codes read, verified, and aggregated to carton and pallet for UDI/regulatory track-and-trace compliance.
sol.identification-traceability.case4.me: UHF RFID handhelds bulk-read tagged cartons and pallets at dock doors, reconciling loads against ASN without line-of-sight scanning.
sol.identification-traceability.cta.desc: Send us your hardest marks, line speed, and surfaces. We will scope readers, mounting, and the genealogy data flow into your MES/ERP — and validate read rate on your parts.
sol.warehouse-logistics.name: Warehouse & Logistics Automation
sol.warehouse-logistics.seoTitle: Warehouse & Logistics Automation | Mobile Data Capture & RFID | GYUTRON
sol.warehouse-logistics.seoDesc: GYUTRON automates receiving, put-away, picking, and shipping with rugged Android PDAs, UHF RFID handhelds, and barcode scanners — real-time WMS sync, 99.9% inventory accuracy, and second-level cycle counts across the DC.
sol.warehouse-logistics.hero.title: Stop guessing what's in the rack
sol.warehouse-logistics.hero.desc: Paper picklists and after-shift batch uploads leave inventory wrong and orders short. GYUTRON puts rugged scanning at every touch — receiving, put-away, picking, shipping — so every move is confirmed at the source and the WMS stays live.
sol.warehouse-logistics.hero.panelDesc: Scan-confirmed at every touch — receiving through dispatch — with real-time WMS sync, not end-of-shift batch reconciliation.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.title: Rugged capture at every touch point
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.intro: One device family covers barcode, RFID, and guided task flow from the dock door to the freezer aisle — built for drops, gloves, frost, and full-shift use.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.1.title: Guided mobile workflows
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.1.desc: Rugged Android PDAs run directed put-away, pick paths, and scan-to-confirm tasks. IP65, 1.5 m drop, -20°C freezer-rated, full-shift battery.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.2.title: First-pass scan reliability
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.2.desc: 1D/2D and DPM engines clear damaged, frosted, and low-contrast labels at 99.5%+ first read — long-range to 12 m for rack-high pallets.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.3.title: Bulk RFID inventory
sol.warehouse-logistics.cap.3.desc: UHF handhelds read 600+ tags/s for cycle counts, dock-door dispatch, and pallet verification — count a bay without touching a carton.
sol.warehouse-logistics.arch.title: Receive -> Move -> Verify -> Sync
sol.warehouse-logistics.arch.intro: A closed-loop data path that confirms every unit against the inventory of record — from inbound dock to outbound truck — with no batch upload in between.
sol.warehouse-logistics.arch.1.title: Receive
sol.warehouse-logistics.arch.1.desc: Inbound scan captures ASN, lot, and quantity against the PO at the dock; discrepancies flagged before stock enters the building.
sol.warehouse-logistics.arch.2.title: Move

sol.warehouse-logistics.arch.3.title: Verify

sol.warehouse-logistics.arch.4.title: Sync

sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.title: From inbound dock to outbound truck
sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.intro: Four scan-confirmed stages replace paper and tribal knowledge — each step writes to the WMS the moment it happens.
sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.1.title: Receiving
sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.1.desc: Scan inbound against ASN/PO; capture lot, serial, and quantity. Over/short/damage flagged at the dock, not at month-end count.
sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.2.title: Put-Away
sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.2.desc: PDA directs stock to the optimal slot; operator scans the location to confirm — eliminating blind placement and lost pallets.
sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.3.title: Pick & Pack

sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.4.title: Shipping
sol.warehouse-logistics.wf.4.desc: Load scan validates order, carrier, and dock door; RFID dock-door reads confirm full-pallet dispatch and close the order.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cases.title: Where it runs
sol.warehouse-logistics.cases.intro: Representative deployments across distribution, cold chain, and contract logistics.
sol.warehouse-logistics.case1.title: Freezer-aisle data capture
sol.warehouse-logistics.case1.metric: -20°C-rated rugged PDAs and anti-condensation scanners capture lot, temperature, and expiry through frost without screen lag.
sol.warehouse-logistics.case2.title: Multi-client cycle counting
sol.warehouse-logistics.case2.metric: UHF RFID handhelds bulk-count mixed pallets at second-level speed, keeping per-client inventory accuracy auditable across tenants.
sol.warehouse-logistics.case3.title: High-velocity pick & pack
sol.warehouse-logistics.case3.metric: Wearable ring scanners and guided pick paths drive hands-free picking with scan-confirmed accuracy at peak-season throughput.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cs.title: Regional DC rollout for a 3PL operator
sol.warehouse-logistics.cs.challenge: A 40,000-pallet distribution center ran paper picklists with end-of-shift data entry. Inventory accuracy sat near 96%, mis-ships drove chargebacks, and quarterly counts shut down two aisles for a full day.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cs.approach: Phased rollout of ~250 rugged Android PDAs and UHF RFID handhelds across receiving, put-away, picking, and shipping. Directed task flow and scan-to-confirm wired into the existing WMS over the site Wi-Fi/5G network; RFID dock-door portals added at outbound.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cs.result: Inventory accuracy reached 99.9%, mis-ship chargebacks fell sharply, and cycle counting moved to rolling RFID sweeps — no aisle shutdowns. Pick productivity rose with guided paths and first-pass scan reliability.
sol.warehouse-logistics.cta.title: Scope your warehouse rollout
sol.warehouse-logistics.cta.desc: Send your facility size, SKU profile, WMS, and target throughput. GYUTRON will recommend a PDA, RFID, and scanner stack with a pilot path and ROI estimate.
sol.industrial-sensing.name: Industrial Sensing & Condition Monitoring
sol.industrial-sensing.seoDesc: Presence, position, and laser distance sensing plus environmental and condition monitoring on IO-Link. Micron-class repeatability, IP67 sensors, and edge connectivity from one vendor.
sol.industrial-sensing.hero.title: Blind machines miss the part
sol.industrial-sensing.hero.desc: Presence checks that drop out in glare. Distance feedback that drifts. Cabinets that overheat with no warning. GYUTRON combines proximity, photoelectric, and laser sensing with environmental and condition monitoring, all reporting over IO-Link to your edge controller.
sol.industrial-sensing.hero.panelDesc: Laser displacement feedback at up to 8 kHz, with stable presence and distance signals on IP67 hardware.
sol.industrial-sensing.cap.title: From part presence to machine health
sol.industrial-sensing.cap.intro: One sensing layer spanning detection physics, non-contact measurement, and continuous condition monitoring, wired and addressed the same way.
sol.industrial-sensing.cap.1.title: Proximity & Presence

sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.title: Capture -> Locate -> Plan -> Guide
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.intro: A deterministic pipeline runs every cycle on a GYUTRON edge controller: image the bin, find each part, plan a reachable grasp, hand coordinates to the robot — closed-loop, under 0.4 s.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.1.title: 1. Capture
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.1.desc: Structured-light 3D head and area-scan camera image the full bin. HDR fusion and noise filtering build a dense, gap-free point cloud of stacked parts.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.2.title: 2. Locate
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.2.desc: CAD/template matching and 3D segmentation isolate each instance and compute its 6-DoF pose. Confidence scoring rejects partial or occluded candidates.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.3.title: 3. Plan
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.3.desc: Grasp ranking selects a reachable, collision-free pick. Bin walls, neighbor parts, gripper geometry, and robot reach are checked before any motion.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.4.title: 4. Guide
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.4.desc: Approach, pick, retract, and place coordinates stream to the robot in its base frame. Failed picks trigger automatic re-imaging and the next-best grasp.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.wf.title: Trained on your part, not your fixture
sol.vision-guided-robotics.wf.intro: Drop in the CAD model, calibrate camera-to-robot, and run. No mechanical re-tooling when parts or robots change.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.wf.1.title: Load the part model
sol.vision-guided-robotics.wf.1.desc: Import CAD or scan a master part. Define gripper, grasp points, and tolerance. New part numbers added without hardware changes.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.wf.2.title: Hand-eye calibration
sol.vision-guided-robotics.wf.2.desc: Guided routine ties the 3D sensor to the robot base frame. Sub-0.1 mm registration. Repeatable after any cell move or service.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.wf.3.title: Run & monitor

sol.vision-guided-robotics.case3.metric: Pick mixed SKUs from totes to conveyor at > 99% success, > 700 picks/hour, vacuum and finger grippers.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.title: Random bin picking of shiny machined parts for CNC loading
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.challenge: A precision machining shop fed CNC lathes by hand from bins of chrome-plated steel shafts dumped in random orientations. The reflective, partially overlapping parts defeated an earlier 2D-only system, and hard tooling could not handle 40+ part variants with frequent changeovers. Manual loading capped uptime and exposed operators to sharp, oily stock.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.approach: A GYUTRON cell paired a blue-light structured-light 3D head with an area-scan camera and a robot-mounted edge controller. HDR multi-exposure capture recovered clean point clouds off the chrome surfaces; CAD matching computed 6-DoF poses, and grasp planning checked bin walls, neighbor parts, and gripper clearance before each pick. Coordinates streamed to the existing robot in its base frame, with automatic re-imaging on any missed grasp.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.result: Sustained > 99.5% first-attempt pick success across the bin, including the last layer, at a 0.38 s locate-and-plan cycle and < 0.1 mm pose accuracy. Changeover to a new part variant dropped from hours of re-fixturing to a CAD import. The cell ran lights-out across two shifts, freeing operators from manual loading and lifting CNC utilization.

sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.title: Mobile manipulators feeding 3C and semiconductor lines
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.challenge: Reels, trays, and carriers move between SMT feeders, test handlers, and stockers by manual cart. Operators walk parts station to station, mis-loads stop the line, and there is no per-carrier record of who touched what or when.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.approach: A mobile manipulator -- AMR base, cobot arm, 3D area-scan head, and on-arm code reader -- drives to each station and docks at +/-100 m. The 3D head locates the carrier and corrects for docking offset; the arm picks to +/-0.05 mm. The on-arm reader confirms the DPM or 2D code before release, and the edge controller reports pose, identity, and AMR position to MES every cycle.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.result: Hands-free transfer between tools, <8 s per pick-and-place, 99.5% first-pass code read, and zero wrong-slot loads released. Full chain-of-custody per carrier; one fleet tends multiple lines instead of operators walking parts.

ind.elec.name: Electronics & Semiconductor Manufacturing
ind.elec.intro: Machine vision and inspection across the electronics chain — 3C consumer electronics, PCB/PCBA, SMT and EMS lines, and semiconductor packaging.
ind.elec.detail: GYUTRON vision, code reading, and 3D measurement run the full electronics line — solder-paste, placement, and reflow inspection (SPI, AOI, AXI), plus module, housing, and final-assembly checks. High-resolution imaging and DPM / 2D code reading give board- and unit-level traceability from incoming components to end of line.

Built for highly reflective metal, fast takt time, and the geometry-critical checks new-energy lines demand.

ind.logi.intro: Robotics and identification for intralogistics — AGV/AMR fleets, smart warehousing, sortation, and in-plant material flow.
ind.logi.detail: AGV/AMR fleets, fixed and handheld code readers, and vision keep intralogistics moving — autonomous transport, smart warehousing, sortation, and palletizing. Rugged data capture keeps receiving, picking, and shipping accurate, with full visibility across in-plant material flow.

ind.gen.tagline: Machining, packaging, and non-standard line automation.
ind.gen.intro: Inspection and automation for general manufacturing — machining, metal and plastic parts, packaging, food & beverage, and non-standard line upgrades.

Modular cameras, lighting, and controllers drop into new lines or upgrade existing ones — including non-standard automation.

iep.cs.challenge: A high-mix SMT line with fine-pitch BGA and QFN let solder defects and hidden voids escape 2D-only checks to functional test.
